mcqs.question: According to Flynn's Taxonomy, a modern GPU is most accurately classified as which type of architecture?

options.text: SIMD
MIMD
SISD
MISD

mcqs.explanation: GPUs execute the same instruction (e.g., add, multiply) on multiple data elements (e.g., pixels, vertices) simultaneously, which is the hallmark of Single Instruction, Multiple Data (SIMD) architecture.

mcqs.question: In a shared-memory system with multiple processor caches, the 'cache coherence' problem primarily refers to:

options.text: The speed difference between cache and main memory
The need to ensure all caches have a consistent view of shared data
The limited size of processor caches
The algorithm used to decide which data to keep in cache

mcqs.explanation: Cache coherence is the challenge of maintaining consistency of shared data stored in multiple local caches. Without a protocol, one processor's update might not be visible to others holding a stale copy.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a key ADVANTAGE of distributed-memory architectures over shared-memory architectures?

options.text: Easier to program due to a unified memory space
Lower latency for all memory accesses
Generally higher scalability to a large number of processors
Automatic avoidance of race conditions

mcqs.explanation: Distributed-memory systems avoid the memory bandwidth bottleneck and cache coherence overhead of large shared-memory systems, allowing them to scale to thousands of processors more effectively.

mcqs.question: In the MESI cache coherence protocol, what does the 'E' (Exclusive) state signify about a cache line?

options.text: The line is dirty and differs from main memory
The line is clean and may also be present in other caches
The line is clean and this is the only copy in any cache
The line is invalid and must be fetched from memory

mcqs.explanation: The Exclusive (E) state means the cache line is clean (identical to main memory) and, crucially, no other caches hold a copy of this line. It is owned exclusively by this cache.

mcqs.question: A computer cluster where each node has its own private memory and communicates via message passing is an example of which memory architecture?

options.text: Non-Uniform Memory Access (NUMA)
Uniform Memory Access (UMA)
Distributed Memory
Virtual Shared Memory

mcqs.explanation: A cluster composed of independent nodes, each with local memory, is the canonical example of a distributed-memory system. Communication must be handled explicitly through messages.
